title: Physiological Sigh
blurb: The fastest way to calm down in real time.
lead: The physiological sigh — a double inhale followed by a long exhale — is one of the quickest evidence-based ways to lower stress in the moment.

blocks.text: A physiological sigh is a deep inhale through the nose, a second short 'top-up' inhale, then a long, slow exhale through the mouth.

It reinflates tiny air sacs in the lungs and offloads carbon dioxide, quickly shifting the nervous system toward calm. Research suggests a few cyclic sighs can measurably reduce stress and improve mood.

items: Inhale through your nose to fill your lungs.
At the top, take a second short sip of air to fully expand them.
Slowly exhale all the air out through your mouth.
Repeat 1–3 times for quick relief, or several minutes for a deeper reset.

items.label: Best for
items.value: Acute stress, anxiety spikes, resetting quickly

blocks.text: Rushing the exhale. The long, slow out-breath is what triggers the calming response.

resources.title: How breathing affects stress
resources.whyHere: Clear explanations of the physiological sigh and cyclic sighing.
